description: With device compliance policies in Microsoft Intune, you can define the rules and requirements that devices must meet to be considered compliant. For example, you can specify a minimum operating system version. This task deploys policy settings that help safeguard tenant data.
userImpact: Windows 10 and later devices that don't meet the specified requirements are marked as noncompliant. When device compliance policies are enforced, noncompliant devices cannot be used to access tenant data.

managementTemplateSteps.displayName: Configure a device compliance policy for Windows 10 and later
managementTemplateSteps.description: With device compliance policies in Microsoft Intune, you can define the rules and requirements that devices must meet to be considered compliant. For example, you can specify a minimum operating system version. This task deploys policy settings that help safeguard tenant data.
managementTemplateSteps.userImpact: Windows 10 and later devices that don't meet the specified requirements are marked as noncompliant. When device compliance policies are enforced, noncompliant devices cannot be used to access tenant data.

informationLinks.displayName: Protect data and devices with Microsoft Intune

versions.name: DeviceCompliance-1

settings.name: displayName
settings.value: M365 Lighthouse - Setup Compliance Policy for Windows 10 devices

referenceStrings.name: Groups_AllGroups
referenceStrings.displayName: A valid Windows version string is required.

lambdaDefinitions.value: select 'M365 Lighthouse - Setup Compliance Policy for Windows 10 devices'

values:
@sys.validateDeviceCompliancePolicy([POST /echo \ {
                "@odata.type": "microsoft.graph.windows10CompliancePolicy",
                "displayName": "[@displayName()]",
                "osMinimumVersion": "[@osMinimumVersion()]",
                "storageRequireEncryption": [@storageRequireEncryption()],
                "activeFirewallRequired": [@activeFirewallRequired()],
                "defenderEnabled": [@defenderEnabled()],
                "rtpEnabled": [@rtpEnabled()],
                "antivirusRequired": [@antivirusRequired()],
                "antiSpywareRequired": [@antiSpywareRequired()],
                "passwordRequired": false,
                "passwordBlockSimple": false,
                "passwordRequiredToUnlockFromIdle": false,
                "passwordRequiredType": "deviceDefault",
                "requireHealthyDeviceReport": false,
                "earlyLaunchAntiMalwareDriverEnabled": false,
                "bitLockerEnabled": false,
                "secureBootEnabled": false,
                "codeIntegrityEnabled": false,
                "signatureOutOfDate": false,
                "deviceThreatProtectionEnabled": false,
                "deviceThreatProtectionRequiredSecurityLevel": "unavailable",
                "configurationManagerComplianceRequired": false,
                "tpmRequired": false,
                "assignments":
                    [[
                        [@sys.join([@sys.concat([@getIncludeGroups([@sys.jsonArrayParse([@includeGroups()])])],[@getExcludeGroups([@sys.jsonArrayParse([@excludeGroups()])])])])]
                    ]]
            }],[@sys.read("policies")],[@sys.read("allUsers")])

managementTemplateSteps.displayName: Set up notifications for noncompliant devices?
managementTemplateSteps.description: When Microsoft Intune detects that a managed device is not in compliance with configured policies, the owner of the device will receive an e-mail with instructions on how to remedy the issue.
managementTemplateSteps.userImpact: Windows 10 and later devices that don't meet the specified requirements are marked as noncompliant. When device compliance policies are enforced, noncompliant devices cannot be used to access tenant data.

informationLinks.displayName: Protect data and devices with Microsoft Intune

versions.name: NotificationForNonCompliantDevices

settings.name: messageTemplate
settings.value: A device assigned to you is out-of-compliance.

lambdaDefinitions.value:
select coalesce(group_concat(x),'none')
                    FROM
                    (
                        select case when data like 'true' then 'includeCompanyLogo' else NULL end as x from arg0
                        union
                        select case when data like 'true' then 'includeCompanyName' else NULL end as x from arg1
                        union
                        select case when data like 'true' then 'includeContactInformation' else NULL end as x from arg2
                        union
                        select case when data like 'true' then 'includeCompanyPortalLink' else NULL end as x from arg3
                    )
select 'A device assigned to you is out-of-compliance.'
